name: Pate Diana
recipeInstructions:
1. Soften 10 tablespoons butter and set aside.
2. Melt 3 tablespoons butter in a skillet. Add onions and shallots. Cook until tender, stirring about 5 minutes.
3. Add chopped apples and cook until tender, about 3 minutes.
4. Transfer apple mixture to a blender.
5. Melt remaining 3 tablespoons butter in the skillet. Add chicken livers and stir for 3 to 4 minutes until browned on the outside and pink inside.
6. Add wine and cook and stir for 2 more minutes.
7. Transfer liver mixture to the blender with the apple mixture. Add 2 tablespoons cream and blend at high speed until smooth. Add more cream, if necessary, to make the mixture smooth.
8. Press through a medium-fine strainer into a mixing bowl. Cool thoroughly, stirring once or twice.
9. In another bowl, cream the remaining 10 tablespoons softened butter with an electric mixer. Add liver mixture a little at a time, beating well after each addition.
10. Stir in lemon juice, salt, and pepper. Pour pate into a crock or ramekins, smoothing the top with a spatula.
11. Pour enough clarified butter over the top to form a seal. Store in the refrigerator up to 1 week or freeze, in appropriate containers, if desired. Serve chilled and garnished with apple slices, if desired.
